basics.name: webAppName
basics.label: Web App Name
basics.toolTip: Pre-filled as 'zappermft' + UTC yyyymmddHHMMSS for uniqueness and can be edited as per your requirement.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 2–60 chars. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.

basics.name: appServicePlanSku
basics.label: App Service Plan SKU
basics.toolTip: Select the pricing tier for your App Service Plan. Basic (B1-B3) for development/testing, Standard (S1-S3) for production workloads, Premium (P1v2-P3v3) for high-performance applications.

basics.name: acrName
basics.label: ACR Name
basics.toolTip: Name of your Azure Container Registry (without .azurecr.io)

validations.message: ACR name must be 5-50 lowercase alphanumeric characters.

basics.name: acrResourceGroup
basics.label: ACR Resource Group
basics.toolTip: Resource group where the ACR is deployed

validations.message: Resource group name: 1-90 characters, alphanumeric with ._()-

basics.name: acrSubscriptionId
basics.label: ACR Subscription ID
basics.toolTip: Subscription ID of the ACR. Hidden because deployment picks current subscription automatically.

validations.message: Must be a valid subscription GUID or empty.

basics.name: acrPullIdentityResourceId
basics.label: User Assigned Managed Identity for ACR (optional)
basics.toolTip: Resource ID of a user-assigned managed identity that already has AcrPull role on the specified ACR. Provide this to allow cross-subscription image pulls without additional role assignment.

validations.message: Must be a valid Azure Resource ID or empty.

basics.name: acrPullIdentityClientId
basics.label: User Assigned Managed Identity Client ID for ACR (optional)
basics.toolTip: Client ID (application ID) of the user-assigned managed identity used for pulling images. If you provide a resource ID above, also provide its client ID here to ensure seamless authentication across subscriptions. Leave blank if not applicable.

validations.message: Must be a valid GUID format or empty.

basics.name: imageName
basics.label: Container Image Name (repository)
basics.toolTip: Name of the container image (e.g. myapp)

validations.message: Image name: 1-256 characters, lowercase alphanumeric with ._/-
Image tag: 1-128 characters, alphanumeric with ._-

basics.name: imageSourceType
basics.label: Image Source

allowedValues.label: Image already exists in target ACR

basics.toolTip: Select 'existing' if the container image is already present in the target registry. Choose 'import' to copy the image from a different registry during deployment.

basics.name: sourceAcrName
basics.label: Source ACR Name
basics.toolTip: Name of the source Azure Container Registry (without .azurecr.io) from which to import the image

validations.message: ACR name must be 5-50 lowercase alphanumeric characters.

basics.name: sourceAcrResourceGroup
basics.label: Source ACR Resource Group
basics.toolTip: Resource group of the source registry

validations.message: Resource group name: 1-90 characters, alphanumeric with ._()-

basics.name: sourceAcrSubscriptionId
basics.label: Source ACR Subscription ID
basics.toolTip: Subscription ID that hosts the source registry

validations.message: Must be a valid subscription GUID.

basics.name: sourceImageName
basics.label: Source Image Name (repository)
basics.toolTip: Repository name of the source image

validations.message: Image name: 1-256 characters, lowercase alphanumeric with ._/-

basics.name: sourceImageTag
basics.label: Source Image Tag
basics.toolTip: Image tag on the source registry

validations.message: Image tag: 1-128 characters, alphanumeric with ._-

basics.name: sourceAcrUsername
basics.label: Source ACR Username (optional)
basics.toolTip: Username for the source registry if admin user or credentials are required. Leave blank if using identity‑based access.

validations.message: Username: up to 256 characters, alphanumeric with ._- or empty

basics.name: sourceAcrPassword
basics.label: Source ACR Password (optional)
basics.toolTip: Password for the source registry if admin user or credentials are required. Leave blank if using identity‑based access.

validations.message: Password: up to 256 characters or empty

options.text:
QUICK CHECK: Azure Resource Providers

Zapper needs 14 Azure resource providers to work its magic.

REQUIRED PROVIDERS:
Microsoft.Solutions • Microsoft.Resources • Microsoft.KeyVault • Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL • Microsoft.Web • Microsoft.ContainerRegistry • Microsoft.ManagedIdentity • Microsoft.Network • Microsoft.Compute • Microsoft.Storage • Microsoft.OperationalInsights • Microsoft.Insights • Microsoft.App • Microsoft.EventGrid

NEED TO REGISTER ONE? Use Azure CLI:
az provider register --namespace name --subscription sub-id

For more details, visit: https://zapperedge.com/preparing-subs-for-zapper-mft-deployment

elements.name: zapperEncryptionKey
elements.label: ZAPPER_JWT_SECRET
elements.toolTip: Encryption key used by the app

validations.message: Encryption key: 8-256 characters required

elements.name: zapperInitialUser
elements.label: User Email Id
elements.toolTip: Email address for the first admin user who will manage Zapper. This user gets full system access to create organizations, manage users, and configure storage. You can add more admin users and modify this email later from within the application.

elements.name: zapperInitialSuperOrgName
elements.label: User Org Name
elements.toolTip: Your organization name (e.g., 'Contoso Corp', 'IT Department'). This creates the initial organization where your first admin user will belong. You can rename this organization or create additional organizations later from within the application. Up to 32 characters: letters, digits, spaces, underscores or hyphens.

constraints.validationMessage: Use up to 32 chars: letters, digits, spaces, underscores or hyphens.

options.text:
DATABASE CONFIGURATIONS

Zapper stores user accounts, permissions, file metadata, and activity logs in PostgreSQL. You have two flexible options:

OPTION 1: Use Existing Database.
✅ Connect to your own PostgreSQL server (on-premises, Azure, AWS, etc.)
✅  Requires providing a connection string: postgres://user:password@host:5432/dbname

OPTION 2: Create New Database 
✅ Azure creates a managed PostgreSQL Flexible Server for you
.

elements.name: databaseOption
elements.label: Database Option
elements.toolTip: Choose whether to connect to an existing PostgreSQL database or create a new Azure-managed PostgreSQL Flexible Server.

elements.name: databaseUrl
elements.label: Existing PostgreSQL URL
elements.toolTip: Full connection string (postgres://user:password@host:5432/dbname)

validations.message: PostgreSQL URL format: postgres://user:password@host:port/database or empty

elements.name: pgServerName
elements.label: PostgreSQL Server Name
elements.toolTip: Name of the new PostgreSQL server

validations.message: Server name: 3-63 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Must start and end with letter/number.

elements.name: pgDatabaseName
elements.label: PostgreSQL Database Name
elements.toolTip: Name of the new database

validations.message: Database name: 1-63 characters, start with letter, alphanumeric with underscores.

elements.name: dbAdminUser
elements.label: PostgreSQL Admin Username
elements.toolTip: Administrator username for the new server

validations.message: Username: 1-63 characters, start with letter, alphanumeric with underscores.

elements.name: dbAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Administrator password for the new server. Must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, and numbers.

constraints.validationMessage: Password must be at least 8 characters and contain uppercase, lowercase, and numbers.

options.text:
ENTERPRISE SSO Integration

Zapper provides pre-configured Microsoft Entra ID credentials for instant deployment. After your app is live, we'll complete the authentication setup for you—no complex configuration needed!

WHAT HAPPENS NEXT:
1. Deploy your app using the pre-filled Entra ID settings below
2. Once deployed, your Web app URL will be: https://[your-app-name].azurewebsites.net
3. Email Web App  URL to support@zapperedge.com (Subject: "Redirect URL for [your-app-name]")
4. Our team will configure the authentication within 12 hours
5. You'll receive a confirmation email when ready

FOR PRODUCTION- USE YOUR OWN ENTRA ID. Simply replace the pre-filled values below with your own Microsoft Entra ID  details.

RESOURCES:
• Zapper Configuration Guide: https://zapperedge.com/entra-configuration-guide
•

elements.name: zapperMsalClientId
elements.label: MSAL Client ID
elements.toolTip: Azure AD application (client) ID for MSAL authentication

validations.message: Must be a valid GUID format (e.g., 1c05ac21-a7ab-4815-84fb-41c2c6e277f8).

elements.name: zapperMsalAuthority
elements.label: MSAL Authority
elements.toolTip: Authority URL for MSAL authentication. Using Zapper's pre-configured values? Remember to email your app URL to support@zapperedge.com after deployment for redirect URI configuration.

validations.message: Must be a valid authority URL starting with https://

elements.name: zapperMsalKnownAuthorities
elements.label: MSAL Known Authorities
elements.toolTip: Comma-separated list of known login domains for MSAL

validations.message: Comma-separated domains: alphanumeric with dots and hyphens.

elements.name: zapperDomainHint
elements.label: MSAL Domain Hint (optional)
elements.toolTip: Optional domain hint for MSAL sign-in (e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com). Leave blank to omit.

validations.message: Domain: alphanumeric with dots and hyphens, or empty.

options.text:
SECURE KEY MANAGEMENT WITH AZURE KEY VAULT

Key Vault is Azure's secure vault for storing sensitive credentials like database passwords. Here's how Zapper uses it:

HOW IT WORKS:
1️⃣ Your database connection string (including password) is stored in Key Vault
2️⃣ Zapper reads it securely at runtime using Managed Identity (no passwords in code!)
3️⃣ Secrets are encrypted, audited, and rotatable without redeploying app

RECOMMENDED: Yes (Key Vault)
✅ Production-grade security—credentials never exposed in app settings
✅ Easy secret rotation—update Key Vault, app auto-picks up changes
✅ Compliance-ready—full audit trail of who accessed secrets when

ALTERNATIVE: No (Environment Variable)
⚠️ Database URL stored as plain app setting (visible in Azure portal)
⚠️ Good for testing/dev environments only

elements.name: useKeyVaultAtRuntime
elements.label: App reads DB URL from Key Vault at runtime?
elements.toolTip: Choose 'No' for testing with an environment variable.

elements.name: kvOption
elements.label: Key Vault Option
elements.toolTip: Select whether to create a new Azure Key Vault for storing secrets or use an existing Key Vault.

elements.name: existingKeyVault
elements.label: Select Existing Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Choose an existing Key Vault from your subscription to store database connection strings and other secrets.

elements.name: existingKeyVaultId
elements.label: Existing Key Vault Resource ID (auto-filled)
elements.toolTip: Auto-filled from the selector. Only paste if cross-subscription/tenant selection is not available.

validations.message: Must be a valid Azure Key Vault Resource ID or empty.

elements.name: existingKvTenantId
elements.label: Key Vault Tenant ID (optional, only if different)
elements.toolTip: Provide the vault's tenant ID if it differs from this subscription's tenant.

validations.message: Must be a valid tenant GUID or empty.

elements.name: newKeyVaultName
elements.label: New Key Vault Name
elements.toolTip: 3–24 chars, lowercase letters, numbers or hyphens.

constraints.validationMessage: Vault name must be 3–24 lowercase letters, numbers or hyphens.

elements.name: dbSecretName
elements.label: Secret Name for DB URL
elements.toolTip: Secret name inside Key Vault to store the connection string

validations.message: Secret name: 1-127 characters, alphanumeric with hyphens.

options.text:
AI AGENT FEATURE - MANUAL FILE PROCESSING

Enable AI Agent to process files on-demand in your storage accounts.

How it works:
1. User selects file → Clicks "Run AI Agent" in Zapper UI
2. Processor calls your AI service API → Sends file for processing
3. Results saved immediately → Output stored in aiagent_results/ directory

Perfect for manual file analysis, on-demand AI processing, and user-initiated operations.
Leave disabled if you don't need AI processing capabilities.

elements.name: enableAIAgent
elements.label: Enable AI Agent Feature?
elements.toolTip: Enable AI-powered file processing capabilities. Users can select files and trigger on-demand AI processing through the Zapper UI.

elements.name: aiAgentResultsDir
elements.label: AI Agent Results Directory
elements.toolTip: Folder name where AI Agent processing results will be saved. Default: aiagent_results

constraints.validationMessage: Use only letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens.
